description: browser-use (web browsing agent) harness + three open web-agent benchmarks (WebVoyager 2025 / Online-Mind2Web / BU Bench V1 open reconstruction) as reusable recipes. The agent drives its own headless Chromium inside a BYO image (build ./Dockerfile) and reports its final answer on stdout; grading is judge-over-final-answer. Pure data — no core changes. v1.1.0: recipes also recover the agent's own step count from the stdout result block (text-metric → agent_steps). v1.2.0: adds bu-bench-v1-open — an open, browseable reconstruction of browser-use's own BU Bench V1 (the official 100 hand-picked tasks ship encrypted, so this samples its five source components: Custom / WebBench / Mind2Web-2 / GAIA / BrowseComp), tagged per source component.

harnesses.description: browser-use 0.13.3 — BYO image (build ./Dockerfile as browseruse-eval:0.13.3) + an OpenAI-compatible model endpoint (LiteLLM). Swap pins.model / env.OPENAI_API_BASE for your deployment.

benchmarkRecipes.description: WebVoyager 2025 revalidated live-web tasks (start URL embedded in the task text) mapped for self-browsing agents: prompt env + judge-over-final-answer + steps.

config.rubric: PASS only if the agent's reported final result concretely and specifically accomplishes the task as stated (correct site, the requested item/fact/action, plausible and grounded in the run log). FAIL if the result is missing, generic, self-reports failure, reports an error/blocker (e.g. CAPTCHA, page unreachable), or contradicts any explicit task constraint (rating, count, date, price, etc.).

benchmarkRecipes.description: Online-Mind2Web (OSU NLP) — 300 live-website tasks, difficulty-labelled — mapped for self-browsing agents: prompt env (start URL appended to the task), per-case difficulty-aware judge rubric + steps.

origin.taskType: live-web task completion on real websites

config.rubric: Task difficulty: {level}. Reference human trajectory length: {reference_length} steps. PASS only if the agent's reported final result shows the task on {website} was concretely completed as stated (the requested navigation/filter/extraction actually happened, grounded in the run log — not just intent). FAIL if the result is missing, generic, self-reports failure, hits a blocker (CAPTCHA/login wall/unreachable page), or violates an explicit constraint in the task.

benchmarkRecipes.description: BU Bench V1 (open reconstruction) — browser-use's own composite benchmark is 100 hand-picked tasks drawn from five sources (20 Custom + 20 WebBench + 20 Mind2Web-2 + 20 GAIA + 20 BrowseComp), scored by an LLM judge. The official set ships ENCRYPTED (BU_Bench_V1.enc) and is not distributable, so this recipe is an open, browseable stand-in that samples the same five components on bot-friendly info-seeking sites; each case is tagged with its source component. Import with opts.text = @bu_bench_v1_open.jsonl.

origin.authors: Browser Use (composite of WebBench, Mind2Web-2/OSU-NLP, GAIA, BrowseComp/OpenAI, + custom tasks)
origin.taskType: live-web task completion + information extraction across five source components (official 100 tasks encrypted; this is an open reconstruction)

config.rubric: BU Bench V1 source component: {source}. Reference answer (may be empty): {answer}. PASS only if the agent's reported final result concretely and correctly accomplishes the task: when a reference answer is given, the result must match it (semantically, ignoring formatting); otherwise the result must be specific, grounded in the run log, and satisfy every explicit task constraint. FAIL if the result is missing, generic, self-reports failure, hits a blocker (CAPTCHA/login wall/unreachable page), or contradicts the task.
